review_title: Great solution to automate SEO for your website
description: Very good. Was easy to deploy and commit over 20,000 recommendations with one click.
author: Ryan M.
pros: We use this for our corporate domain and consistently rank higher for all our keywords. For example, search for “precast concrete miami” or “precast concrete atlanta” and Coreslab is always on top. For most locations its consistent. We don’t need a full time SEO person now. It also does not slow the site down at all. No difference with or without the java code. It made over 20,000 changes instantly to optimize all meta tags, alt txt images etc. Also recommends sits you should post to for backlinks which is useful. I’d recommend it. I’m not affiliated with them, I am the IT Director at Coreslab International but we have used their solution since re-launching their website in March.
cons: Because it uses Java script you have to make sure no plugins for security are blocking the script or it won't work properly. Also have to ensure the Robots.txt is updated to allow it to be scanned.

review_title: Practical, step-by-step, tips I can follow to optimize
author: Arlène V.
pros: There are plenty of SEO platforms on the market but I think there are a couple of things that make Alli AI unique and that I'm a fan of. The first one is the fact that Alli AI does a full analysis of your website and looks at your keywords. It's one thing to think that you have optimized your platform but it's another thing to see an actual "screenshot" of how you have done with your optimization efforts. Then there is my favourite feature: tasks. Imagine having your own SEO expert giving you relevant advice like 'Create an About Page', 'Add the keyword in the alt tag of your images', 'Create more content that revolves around your keyword on your website', and so forth. It goes without saying that some of Alli AI's recommendations are going to be more relevant and important than others, but it's nice to get practical advice that you can follow in a step-by-step manner.
cons: One thing I'd change about Alli AI has to do with the optimization suggestions the platform gives you when it analyses your website. If you have many suggestions, it may be tempting to simply approve them all automatically. Some of them will optimize your website but could have a negative impact on things like your website user experience, for example. I think that ranking suggestions by importance would help solve this because a user would know what to focus on right now and what could be changed at a later date because it isn't that important.
